sections.paragraph:
to be extinguished, quenched, go out; properly spoken of fire, Lev 6:5, of a lamp, 1Sa 3:3 metaph. of the anger of God, 2Ki 22:17 of the destruction of enemies, Isa 43:17, “they are quenched like a wick.” (Arab. كبا to cover a fire with ashes, not quite to extinguish, but خبا is to be extinguished. The primary idea is that of covering over, hiding, compare חָבָא, חָבָה. To this answers the Gr. σβέω.)

Piel, to extinguish, put out, prop. Isa 1:31 42:3 metaph. Jer 4:4 21:12 . 2Sa 21:17, לֹא תְכַבֶּה אֶת־נֵר יִשְׂרָאֵל “that thou quench not the light of Israel,” lest thou, the alone light of the people, shouldest perish. Compare 2Sa 14:7, and above, see גַּחֶלֶת , page 167, B .
